artifacts.summary: 5-phase implementation plan resolving 12 field-level mismatches between Lean JSONL export and Python schema layers

memory_candidates.content: When integrating data exports between Lean 4 and Python, the primary friction is naming convention mismatch: Lean emits camelCase JSON keys while Python dataclasses use snake_case. The most maintainable pattern is to keep Lean idiomatic and build a single Python ingest adapter that handles all field-name translations, rather than modifying the Lean export or scattering translation logic across multiple Python modules. Avoid maintaining parallel schema layers (here data/schema.py vs schema/records.py) -- pick one canonical type and deprecate the other early.
